item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The image could not be reliably sourced, the strongest lead being here. As such, it is assumed not compliant with CC 3.0 and has since been removed.

item.title: Sorry for the necro, but....
item.content:encoded: I didn't see rapey things at all. I figured they took a normal human, and either force fed them a substance from the van, or took them and "built them in" to another vehicle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The coolest parts were expunged. :/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why the DATA EXPUNGED? The researchers don't want other researchers reading the report to know what this item is capable of? So it will be extra spooky to other researchers?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The article itself is kind of meh, and the [REDACTED BUT SUPER SPOOPY GUYZ] at the end convinced me to downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 for the biological fridge-logic. The creature-camouflaged-as-inorganic-object sub-genre is one I generally enjoy, but too many of them have a "*shrug, I dunno" approach to how the creature actually works. The fun of monsters that shouldn't be alive is figuring out how they could be, and the "it works because of reasons"/"black box" approach kills a lot of that fun.

On the subject of black boxes, I want to congratulate you on not even implying rape reproduction; but judging by the comments not every reader is interpreting that redaction the same way. It might help remove that option if Agent Onno was firmly established as male. Or just fill in more of the blanks. "The unfortunate infestation of Agent Onno." leaves less room for undesirable implication drawing. Third option, you could focus and expand on what it does to a vehicle to reproduce, and relegate Agent Onno to a footnote about being considered KIA with post-humus honors.

Edit: Just realized, Agent "OH, NOOOOOO!" if that was intentional, you should be ashamed of yourself (not really). If it's only in my head, it would about par for the course.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So many King works get made into films, it's unreal!

As for the SCP: I liked it wholeheartedly. I upvoted at the idea of modular organisms (what a great phrase!) that take the form of small businesses.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm sort of ambivalent on this one. There isn't really anything to dislike, but it's missing something to get a +1. I'm not precisely sure what that something is, though. It just seems a little short-sold.

Also: Lawnmower Man (the Stephen King short story, not the terrible movie)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would try to add a little meat at the end [as to what, the author knows more about the objects/specimens' go abouts than I].
It kind of just…Ends. Which is meh. And as I've seen one of everything once or more, I still enjoy concepts like this [LLAMA VAN BAY-BEE].
Upvote and hopeful that it expands.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, I forgot about the real-life Freddy Wong. Glad I didn't decide to name him that. You're right about the name, too. Thanks for the input.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Solid upvote from me. It's very creative and creepy and just… strikes a chord for me, I dunno. Maybe its because there's a thousand white vans and Hispanic gardeners around me area and it just perfectly conveys that weird theme of of alien-monster-thing-appearing-to-be-human-god-knows-why-or-how.

Also, I'm glad you didn't stick with the name Freddie Wong, that would have been too much of a jerk back to reality (unless you partially redacted it of course, then it would be a cool subtle nod).

Now some criticism. I would change your final line to an addended researcher note. Its really the perfect level of formal/informal for that, while its a bit too presumptuous/informal for the main description.

The title: "FREDDY SANCHEZ'S SUPER LAWN CARE SERVICE!!" draws a little too much attention to itself for something that's trying to blend in. I realize you were trying to imply that the entity doesn't quite understand human customs 100% right with this title, but I think that could be better served with "odd word use" instead of "super happy fun japanese excitement." (Though if you'd kept Freddie Wong, this would fit well!) I personally might do something like maybe "Freddie Sanchez's Service Super Care Lawn" to show a botched blend-in attempt without trying to draw excitement to itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, dear. It's probably for the best if I change it, then, both for the unfortunate implications and the negative feedback that I've been getting over the expungement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My immediate thought was rape, too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not a big fan of the expungement, but this is really classic and I just love articles like these.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My first thought was those fat creatures from Doctor Who. Still horrifying, but less "Galaxy of Terror" horrifying.

item.title: Re: Mod Post
item.content:encoded: Inkshooter, please don't reply to Staff/Mod/Admin posts if you're not staff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've been getting a lot of mixed responses on the redaction, some good, some bad. My thought was that the SCP forcibly infuses its victims with a biotic material that alters the genome of the host's cells, like a virus.

I don't like or want to write about rape, so if anyone feels like the SCP lends itself to be interpreted in that way, I'll be happy to change it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm confused… I didn't mention latin music at all in the actual piece.

The character's race was purely incidental. I had his name be Freddy Wong originally, but I changed it to Sanchez later on a whim.

Glad you liked it, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not gonna vote on account of the whole "lol reproduction an agent got raepd" part but it's well written enough to not downvote.

item.title: Re: Mod Post
item.content:encoded: Sorry about that, I'll avoid such statements in the future.

item.title: Mod Post
item.content:encoded:
If you know that something is tasteless and offensive, don't post it.

Consider this an official warning. Don't be a shithead in the future.

item.title: Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Ehh… You're toeing the racism line there, buddy. Dial it back.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really didn't like any of the expungement at the end, and most of it fell flat for me. The last line made me downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked the latino taste in this. Feels like home. +1

E: Also, do you realize how wide latino music is? Each nation has its own coutnry music. W have also several genres of music that can be called "latino".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why, Latino music causes it to garden, of course!

*is arrested for tasteless stereotyping*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On the one hand, its a pretty straightforward 'non-organic thing turns out to be organic' body horror piece in some respects, and the reproduction part just seems like borderline sloppy expungement to me. However, there are lots of things I rather like about this, like the idea of an anomalous gardener, the stuff about the gardener moving like an earthworm, and the last line.

Overall, I think its a good article even though its in the same vein as several other similar articles, and although it'd be nice to have some of the expungement cleared up, the rest is good enough for me to overlook it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the use of local color, especially since I listen to KZOK all the time in my car.

Actually, a second thought; considering the formats of those two stations, is there any rhyme or reason to why it's specifically calmed by country music and enraged by classic rock? What happens if it hears a different station with the same format? Considering that it's presenting itself as a Hispanic person, what happens if it's exposed to a Latino music station?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I felt creeped out immediately seeing a picture of a nondescript white van. This was pretty creative. The reproduction part was a nice touch. Great use of redaction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It also serves as SCP-1524's reproductive system, as demonstrated by the multiple different specimens present at the site of recovery and the unfortunate ███████████ of Agent Onno.

Oh god. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It feels like we have a lot of articles like this, but nevertheless it's still well done. Tentative up vote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Didn't find very interesting needs a hook in my opinion

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked the draft, and I like it now. Here's to successing the 1524 slot. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hello, as this stands, it is just a truck that is also organic. It didn't really interest me through the weirdness aspect as the foundation already has several "objects that shouldn't be organic, but are".

Where you put that agitating it makes it [DATA EXPUNGED] and then hinted at what that means, I liked that, but you only have one line of emphasis on it and then the report just abruptly ends.

I feel that there is a problem with tone where you say "it walks with wave-like lurching motions, somewhat like those of an earthworm." The foundation doesn't usually make subjective similes. If it moves a certain way, just state that it moves a certain way.

I'd be willing to change my vote if it had more oomph

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's still a SCP-XXXX in there. Otherwise, pretty cool. +1.
